title: Roku Q1 2026 Earnings Report - Hollywood Reporter
text: Roku delivered strong first-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street expectations across multiple metrics. The streaming platform company reported net revenue of $1.25 billion, exceeding guidance of $1.2 billion and analyst expectations. The company achieved net income of $85.7 million, substantially above its $50 million guidance. Platform revenue grew 28 percent year-over-year to $1.13 billion, driven primarily by advertising revenue of $613 million (up 27 percent) and subscription revenue of $519 million (up 30 percent). Streaming hours reached 38.7 billion, representing an 8 percent increase year-over-year. The company also set an all-time free cash flow record at $538.8 million. The earnings announcement follows Roku's April 16 announcement of reaching over 100 million streaming households worldwide. Additionally, research firm Antenna estimated that Roku's Howdy service surpassed 1 million subscribers after its August launch at $2.99 monthly. Roku raised its full-year guidance, expecting platform revenue growth near 21 percent to approximately $5 billion. Total projected net revenue stands at $5.5 billion. The company stated: "These results affirm our path to sustaining double-digit Platform revenue growth, expanding margins, and growing our north star metric."
